scenarios.name: 200 EMA Golden Cross Scalp

conditions.definition: (close - open) / (high - low) > 0.6

scenarios.notes: Pure EMA trend following. Avoid in choppy/ranging markets. Best in confirmed uptrends.

scenarios.name: EMA Stack Pullback Re-entry

conditions.definition: Price retraces to 21 EMA within 1-3 candles
Close > Open AND (Close - Open) / (High - Low) > 0.6
21 EMA > 50 EMA > 200 EMA within 2% spread

scenarios.notes: High-quality pullback in confirmed uptrend. Requires EMA alignment on 4h.

scenarios.name: 200 EMA Bear Cross Short
scenarios.notes: Mirror of S001. SHORT-biased trend continuation.

scenarios.name: EMA Fan Short Collapse

conditions.definition: 21 < 50 < 200 EMA (bearish stack)
3+ consecutive lower closes on 1h
Close < Open, Body > 60%

scenarios.notes: Strong downtrend with EMA alignment. Institutional-grade short setup.

scenarios.name: Range Breakout

conditions.definition: MAX(close) of last 20 candles (5m)
Current candle close > range_high + 0.1%

scenarios.notes: Classic range extension with volume confirmation. High-frequency setup.

scenarios.name: 4H Coil Breakout

conditions.definition: ATR_14_4h at 52-week low
(BB_upper - BB_lower) / price < 0.008
Close beyond Bollinger Band + volume > 1.5x avg

scenarios.notes: Institutional squeeze breakout. Lower frequency but HIGH edge. Trend direction ambiguous at entry.

scenarios.name: Wedge Breakout

conditions.definition: 2+ higher lows AND lower highs converging
Close beyond wedge apex resistance or support

conditions.bullish: RSI makes higher low while price makes lower low
conditions.bearish: RSI makes lower high while price makes higher high

scenarios.notes: Structural trade with divergence confirmation. HIGH-PROBABILITY institutional setup.

scenarios.name: Volume Climax Reversal

conditions.definition: Volume > MA_volume_30 * 2.5 to 3.5
Candle low 2-3% below high, close near high
MACD line starting to roll over (signal not yet crossed)

scenarios.notes: HIGHEST-WIN-RATE setup. Institutional stop-hunt + reversal pattern. Requires precise timing.

scenarios.name: Weekly Level Break

conditions.definition: Break of weekly chart high or low
Candle close beyond level (not just wick touch)

scenarios.notes: INSTITUTIONAL EDGE. Weekly level breaks are high-probability with large moves. Rare setups.

scenarios.name: VWAP Pullback Long

conditions.definition: Price pullback to VWAP within -1% range
Close > Open, Body > 60% of range

scenarios.notes: High-probability pullback into dynamic support (VWAP). Institutional entry point.

scenarios.name: Support Retest

conditions.definition: Recent swing low or horizontal support
Price touches support ±0.3%
Long wick below support, close > mid-candle
No candle closes permanently below support

scenarios.notes: INSTITUTIONAL FAVORITE. Support retest with volume absorption = high-probability range continuation.

scenarios.name: EMA Pullback Engulfing

conditions.definition: Price pullback to 21 or 50 EMA within 1-2 candles
Current candle body completely contains previous candle body
21 EMA > 50 EMA > 200 EMA
Volume(engulfing) > Volume(previous) × 1.3

scenarios.notes: Pattern confirmation with trend alignment. Engulfing candle adds confidence to pullback entry.

scenarios.name: Order Block Entry

conditions.definition: Strong 1h candle with high volume (Volume > 1.5x average)
Price moves away from OB zone 5-10 candles
Price retests OB zone with bullish reaction
No candle closes beyond opposite side of OB

scenarios.notes: HIGHEST-WIN-RATE pullback setup. Institutional market structure play with clear support zone.

scenarios.name: RSI Reset Pullback

conditions.definition: RSI(14) crosses 50 from above (momentum loss + reset)
21 EMA > 50 EMA > 200 EMA (uptrend),  no breakdown
Next candle after RSI reset close > open
Volume > SMA_volume_20

scenarios.notes: Momentum reset + trend continuation. RSI 50 level is natural pullback zone.

scenarios.name: RSI Divergence Reversal

conditions.definition: 3+ consecutive lower closes or lower highs
RSI makes higher low while price makes lower low
Bullish candle after divergence (close > open, body > 50%)

scenarios.notes: Divergence signals momentum exhaustion. HIGH-EDGE reversal setup.

scenarios.name: Double Top Breakdown

conditions.definition: 2 highs within 0.5% of each other
Price pullback to midpoint between tops
Close below midpoint + no closing back above
Volume on breakdown > volume at formation

scenarios.notes: Classic reversal pattern. Resistance tested twice = likely rejection third time.

scenarios.name: Double Bottom Breakout

conditions.definition: 2 lows within 0.5% of each other
Price bounces to midpoint between bottoms
Close above midpoint + sustained above
Volume on breakout > volume at formation

scenarios.notes: Mirror of S028. Support tested twice = likely support holds third time.

scenarios.name: Exhaustion Candle Reversal

conditions.definition: Gap down open then spike up 2-3% same candle
Large upper wick (2-3x lower wick size)
Close in lower half of candle

scenarios.notes: HIGHEST-WIN-RATE reversal. Stop-hunt pattern with exhaustion = automatic reversal.

scenarios.name: Parabolic Reversal

conditions.definition: Accelerating uptrend: each candle high > previous high, closing gaps accelerate
Volume decreases as price rises (momentum weakening)
First lower close or doji after parabolic move

scenarios.notes: Parabolic moves eventually collapse. Declining volume = exhaustion signal.

scenarios.name: MACD Flip Reversal

conditions.definition: MACD line > signal line, histogram positive
MACD line crosses below signal line (mechanical flip)
Next candle close < open (bearish)
Optional: RSI also rolling over or divergence present

scenarios.notes: Mechanical indicator flip. Works but lower edge than divergence-based entries.

scenarios.name: Trendline Break Reversal

conditions.definition: Uptrend trendline connecting 2+ lower swing lows
Close below trendline (not just wick touch)
Next candle(s) don't recapture above trendline
Volume on break > typical volume

scenarios.notes: INSTITUTIONAL EDGE. Long-term trendline break = major reversal signal. Rare, high-confidence.

scenarios.name: Bollinger Squeeze Breakout

conditions.definition: BB width = (Upper - Lower) / price < 0.8%
Squeeze lasting 3-10 candles
Close beyond BB band (upper or lower) + volume > 1.5x
Next candle continues beyond band

stop_loss.note: Place on opposite side of breakout

scenarios.notes: Volatility expansion play. Lower frequency but consistent edge when squeeze breaks.

scenarios.name: Low ATR Expansion

conditions.definition: ATR_14_4h at historical low (52-week low or percentile < 20)
Multiple lower highs AND higher lows converging
Candle with 2× normal range (high - low) > 2 * ATR_avg
Volume on expansion > 1.8× average

scenarios.notes: Mean reversion of volatility. After low ATR, expansion is highly likely.

scenarios.name: Multi-Timeframe Compression

conditions.definition: 15m ATR at low levels
1h BB width < 1%
All EMAs within 1% spread on 4h
All 3 timeframes showing compression signals
15m candle closes beyond compression on 1h/15m

scenarios.notes: HIGHEST-EDGE squeeze. Multi-TF alignment confirms institutional setup.

scenarios.name: Inside Bar Breakout

conditions.definition: Current candle high < previous candle high AND current low > previous low
Inside bar represents range compression
Next candle close outside previous range (upper or lower)
Volume on breakout > volume on inside bar

scenarios.notes: High-frequency pattern. Simple rule: compression = breakout coming.

scenarios.name: Triangle Break Breakout

conditions.definition: Lower highs AND higher lows converging on 1h over 10-20 candles
Volume decreasing into triangle apex
Close beyond triangle boundary + next candle confirms
Volume on breakout > 1.5× average

scenarios.notes: Structural pattern with predictable breakout. Declining volume = volatility buildup.

scenarios.name: Fakeout Expansion Reversal

conditions.definition: Close beyond compression boundary on 1h then reversal next 1-2 candles
Volume spike on false breakout > 2× average
Strong reversal candle back inside compression zone
Entry on bounce back into range (contrarian)

scenarios.notes: Contrarian / institutional stop-hunt play. High-volume false breakout = trap.

scenarios.name: Equal High Sweep Reversal

conditions.definition: Price touched same high level exactly 2× within 20 candles
Pullback from first high 10-15% move
Rally to equal high again (second test)
Spike 0.2-0.5% beyond equal high then large wick rejection
Strong reversal candle with volume (entry = close of reversal candle)

scenarios.notes: INSTITUTIONAL FAVORITE. Sweep to exact level then reversal = highest-confidence setup.

scenarios.name: Equal Low Sweep Reversal

conditions.definition: Price touched same low level exactly 2× within 20 candles
Bounce from first low 10-15% move
Decline to equal low again (second test)
Spike 0.2-0.5% below equal low then large wick rejection upward
Strong reversal candle downward with volume

scenarios.notes: Mirror of S046. Downside sweep = SHORT setup.

scenarios.name: Stop Hunt Reversal

conditions.definition: 3+ candles trending higher (higher closes)
Sudden spike 2-3% beyond recent trend high
Long wick below spike high + small body (stop-hunt pattern)
Next candle close < open (entry after reversal confirmation)

scenarios.notes: HIGHEST-WIN-RATE setup. Institutional stop-hunt = automatic reversal pattern.

scenarios.name: Liquidity Void Fill

conditions.definition: Gap in volume profile: area with very low trading volume on 50-100 candle lookback
Void location above or below price determines direction
Price trends toward void level
Volume increasing as price approaches void
Close beyond void boundary

scenarios.notes: REQUIRES VOLUME PROFILE DATA. Institutional positioning drives liquidity voids. Defer to Phase 5 if unavailable.

scenarios.name: BOS + Liquidity Grab

conditions.definition: Easily identifiable swing high or swing low on 1h
BOS: close beyond swing point (not just wick)
No candle post-BOS closes back beyond BOS level
Immediate spike 0.5-1.5% opposite direction (grab)
Reversal back in BOS direction (entry after grab + reversal)

scenarios.notes: INSTITUTIONAL EDGE. BOS + liquidity spike = stop-hunt + grab + reversal (highest probability).
